summary: Manage Systems Manager Parameter Store: add a parameter, update a value strictly by reference (never raw secrets), retag and re-describe, change storage tier, and delete with engineer review.

operations.title: Add a new SSM parameter
operations.summary: Create a new parameter, copying the path and tier from an existing one. Secrets must be a pointer, never pasted in directly.
operations.description: Creates a new Parameter Store entry alongside an existing reference parameter, inheriting its path prefix and tier conventions. For SecureString type, the value must be supplied as a pointer to an existing Secrets Manager secret, never a literal secret.

params.name: parameter
params.label: Reference parameter (for path/tier context)
params.help: Pick an existing parameter in the same namespace to copy path prefix and tagging conventions from.

params.name: new_path
params.label: New parameter path

bounds.semantic: full SSM parameter path, must start with /

params.help: Full hierarchical path for the new parameter, e.g. /myapp/prod/app/new_setting.

params.name: type
params.label: Parameter type
params.help: String for plain config, StringList for comma-separated values, SecureString for KMS-encrypted secrets.

params.name: tier
params.label: Parameter tier
params.help: Advanced allows larger values and policies but incurs additional cost.

params.name: new_value_reference
params.label: Value or value pointer

bounds.semantic: for SecureString this must be a Secrets Manager ARN pointer, never a literal secret value; for String/StringList it may be the literal config value

params.help: Plain config value, or for SecureString a pointer (Secrets Manager ARN) to the actual secret.

params.name: description
params.label: Description
params.help: Short human-readable description of what this parameter controls.

params.name: key_id
params.label: KMS key (SecureString only)
params.help: KMS key alias or ARN used to encrypt SecureString values.

operations.title: Update parameter value by reference
operations.summary: Point this parameter at a new value or secret pointer, without ever handling the raw secret here. Consuming apps must re-read it.
operations.description: Repoints an existing parameter's value to a new source of truth without ever handling the raw secret in this workflow. For SecureString parameters, the reference must be a Secrets Manager ARN; consuming applications should be verified to re-read the parameter after the change.

params.name: parameter
params.label: Parameter
params.help: Select the parameter whose value should be updated.

params.name: new_value_reference
params.label: New value or value pointer

bounds.semantic: must be a pointer (Secrets Manager ARN or another SSM path) for SecureString parameters, never a literal secret value

params.help: For SecureString, provide a Secrets Manager ARN pointer. For plain String/StringList, the literal new value is acceptable.

operations.title: Update parameter description
operations.summary: Update this parameter's description text. Documentation only — no effect on consumers.
operations.description: Changes the human-readable description on an existing parameter for documentation purposes only; no functional impact on consumers.

params.name: parameter
params.label: Parameter
params.help: Select the parameter to update.

params.name: new_description
params.label: New description
params.help: Short description of what this parameter is used for.

operations.title: Add or update a parameter tag
operations.summary: Add or update a metadata tag on this parameter. Metadata only — no effect on its value or consumers.
operations.description: Adds a new tag key/value pair or updates an existing one on a parameter for cost allocation and ownership tracking; does not affect the parameter value or consumers.

params.name: parameter
params.label: Parameter
params.help: Select the parameter to tag.

params.name: tag_key
params.label: Tag key
params.help: Tag key, e.g. Environment, Service, CostCentre.

params.name: tag_value
params.label: Tag value
params.help: Tag value to associate with the key.

operations.title: Change parameter tier
operations.summary: Move this parameter between storage tiers. Advanced costs more but allows bigger values; downgrading can fail if it no longer fits.
operations.description: Moves a parameter between Standard, Advanced and Intelligent-Tiering. Advanced tier supports larger values and parameter policies but incurs additional per-parameter cost; downgrading from Advanced can fail if the current value or policy count exceeds Standard limits.

params.name: parameter
params.label: Parameter
params.help: Select the parameter to re-tier.

params.name: new_tier
params.label: New tier
params.help: Advanced costs more and supports larger values (up to 8KB) and policies; Standard is free and capped at 4KB.

operations.title: Delete an SSM parameter
operations.summary: Permanently delete this parameter. This can't be undone, and anything still reading it breaks immediately; an engineer reviews it first.
operations.description: Permanently removes a parameter from Parameter Store. Any application or automation reading this parameter at runtime will fail to resolve it immediately after deletion; confirm no live consumers before proceeding.

params.name: parameter
params.label: Parameter
params.help: Select the parameter to permanently delete.

operations.title: Provision an SSM document
operations.summary: Create the SSM document shell. The content can run arbitrary commands, so it is engineer-authored.
operations.description: Create an aws_ssm_document shell: name, type, and format. The document CONTENT can run arbitrary commands on managed instances, so it is never captured here - it is authored and reviewed by an engineer. Submitting drafts the resource and routes it to an engineer to complete and own.

params.name: document_name
params.label: Document name
params.help: The name of the document (written to the name attribute).

params.name: document_type
params.label: Document type
params.help: Command runs on instances; Automation runs a workflow; Session configures Session Manager; Policy is an inventory or compliance policy.

params.name: document_format
params.label: Document format
params.help: The format the engineer authors the content in.

params.name: content
params.label: Content
params.help: The document content (SSM steps or commands) is authored and reviewed by an engineer, never typed or run from this form, because it can execute arbitrary commands on managed instances.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: Description
params.help: A short description recorded as the resource's Description tag.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: The team or person accountable for this resource, recorded as its PIC tag.

decisions: The document content can run arbitrary commands on managed instances; the content is authored and reviewed by an engineer, never captured or run from this form.
Confirm the document type and target scope: a Command or Automation document executes on whatever it targets, so the target type is confirmed at review.

operations.title: Provision a new SSM parameter
operations.summary: Create a new Parameter Store entry; the value is captured as a pointer only, never a raw secret.
operations.description: Stand up a Parameter Store entry as a complete specification: path, type, and tier. The value itself must be supplied as a pointer to an existing Secrets Manager secret or another SSM parameter, never a literal — the same convention ssm-parameter-add and ssm-parameter-update-value-by-reference already use. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish — nothing is applied without review.

params.name: parameter_name
params.label: Parameter path
params.help: Full hierarchical path, e.g. /myapp/prod/app/new_setting. Names the parameter in the generated draft.

params.name: type
params.label: Parameter type
params.help: String for plain config, StringList for comma-separated values, SecureString for KMS-encrypted secrets.

params.name: value_reference
params.label: Value or value pointer
params.help: A pointer only — a Secrets Manager ARN or another SSM parameter path — never a literal value, matching how this parameter's value is updated afterward (ssm-parameter-update-value-by-reference).

params.name: tier
params.label: Parameter tier
params.help: Advanced allows larger values and parameter policies but incurs additional cost.

params.name: key_id
params.label: KMS key (SecureString only)
params.help: KMS key alias or ARN used to encrypt SecureString values.

params.name: description
params.label: Description
params.help: Short human-readable description of what this parameter controls.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this parameter controls
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this parameter.

decisions: Parameter policies (expiration, expiration notification, NoChangeNotification) and a custom allowed_pattern validation regex are engineer follow-ups after creation.
